title: Ports, Immutable Values, Promise Ownership, and Lifecycle · Retrieval Circuit
summary: A delayed incident review reconstructs ports, immutable values, promise ownership, and lifecycle from revision, authorization, seed, URL, policy, response bytes, parsed identity, record provenance, frontier transition, Promise settlement, browser state, report row, failure, cleanup, and recovery evidence. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for explicitly owned crawler component graph after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case prac…

stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: A review TypeScript team handles case 7381 during a hostile redirect, stream, and checkpoint drill. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to prove port direction, admitted values, Promise and AbortSignal ownership, and handle-clean lifecycle; owner authorization and revocation must remain separate from robots and legal-review claims; then inject one authenticated, private-data, output-injection, path, log, plugin, or correction fault and contain it. Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, Cheerio, Playwright, axe-core, files, processes, packages, containers, assistive-technology review, load, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Inventory streams, sockets, timers, browser contexts, files, listeners, workers, and close order with post-run handle evidence.

lines.label: risk
lines.text: Returning from main proves all resources closed.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Inventory streams, sockets, timers, browser contexts, files, listeners, workers, and close order with post-run handle evidence. Artifact under review: explicitly owned crawler component graph.

options.text: The record names the assumption, applies Handle Ledger, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.
The record says the result looks right but supplies no quantity, trace, condition, or counterexample. That claim does not survive the separate evidence from confidence evidence boundary.

For separate evidence from confidence, fetch one convenient page, omit owner authorization, source and emitted revision, URL and robots policy, runtime admission, stream and Promise ownership, Cheerio and browser differences, accessibility states, changed fixtures, recovery, and controlled Node transfer, then accept plausible objects as TypeScript crawler proof.

steps.title: Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Record what required effort · Review Handle Ledger
steps.instruction:
Write the review evidence note for Handle Ledger: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A review TypeScript team handles case 2524 during an authorized municipal service audit. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to prove port direction, admitted values, Promise and AbortSignal ownership, and handle-clean lifecycle; every external value remains unknown until runtime admission produces an owned value; then change one owner permission, seed, URL, robots, sitemap, redirect, DNS, media, or encoding decision and reconcile provenance. Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, Cheerio, Playwright, axe-core, files, processes, packages, containers, assistive-technology review, load, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Inventory streams, sockets, timers, browser contexts, files, listeners, workers, and close order with post-run handle evidence.

Evidence target: Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Record what required effort · Review Handle Ledger.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Record what required effort · Review Handle Ledger, keep Handle Ledger connected to executable TypeScript authorization, URL, runtime admission, Fetch, Promise, browser, accessibility, report, package, and recovery decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Inventory streams, sockets, timers, browser contexts, files, listeners, workers, and close order with post-run handle evidence.

hints: For Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Record what required effort · Review Handle Ledger, name the owner, authorization, source and emitted revision, Node, TypeScript, URL, robots, unknown value, Fetch, Promise, AbortSignal, browser, accessibility, report, handle, recovery, and native-transfer boundary, Handle Ledger precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Record what required effort · Review Handle Ledger, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: A review TypeScript team handles case 2524 during an authorized municipal service audit. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to prove port direction, admitted values, Promise and AbortSignal ownership, and handle-clean lifecycle; every external value remains unknown until runtime admission produces an owned value; then change one owner permission, seed, URL, robots, sitemap, redirect, DNS, media, or encoding decision and reconcile provenance. Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, Cheerio, Playwright, axe-core, files, processes, packages, containers, assistive-technology review, load, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Inventory streams, sockets, timers, browser contexts, files, listeners, workers, and close order with post-run handle evidence.

For Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Record what required effort · Review Handle Ledger, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: Returning from main proves all resources closed.
